summary: Iter 111 — M-3 CoA per-PT override + Inter-company Transfer JE. All 17 tests PASSED (7 CoA per-PT, 5 IC transfer JE, 5 regression). Both features work end-to-end: template global vs per-PT override isolation (KSC↔KANDA), auto-post balanced JE on inter-entity transfer approve at-cost (Dr IC-AR/Cr Persediaan @ source, Dr Persediaan/Cr IC-AP @ dest), idempotent (2 JE per pair_id), trial balance balanced both PT, intra-entity/rejected transfers do NOT trigger IC JE. Seed idempotent (45 template accounts stable across sync).

minor.issue: Total template accounts = 45 (per DEFAULT_COA in gl_service.py), not 49 as stated in review_request. Consistent with code — likely just documentation drift in the request text; no bug. IC-AR (1-1250) and IC-AP (2-1250) ARE present.

action_items: Optionally update DEFAULT_COA count reference in docs (45 vs 49 discrepancy in review text).

In gl_service.post_intercompany_transfer(), the update_many() call at lines 741-747 uses a placeholder counterpart_entity_id then overwrites per-row afterwards. The intermediate update_many is dead-write and can be simplified — cosmetic only.
critical_code_review_comments: gl_service.post_intercompany_transfer(): the update_many at lines 741-747 sets intercompany_counterpart_entity_id via a `dst if True else src` placeholder then immediately overwritten per-row. Redundant; safe to remove and rely on the two per-row update_one calls.
gl_service.py is 1400+ lines — split into gl_service/, e.g. accounts.py, journal.py, autopost.py, reports.py for maintainability (violates >700 line guideline).
list_accounts() uses in-memory dedupe by code (O(n) merge) — fine for now (<5k accounts) but consider Mongo aggregation for larger tenants.
delete_account for override PT does NOT check whether the override was used to post JEs (posting resolves by-code global, so this is intentional and safe — good design).
seed_data_creation: Ran bash scripts/seed_reset.sh at start; test file creates temporary override accounts (9-KSC-*, 1-1200@KSC, 1-1110@KSC, 1-1100@KANDA) and cleans them up in finally-blocks. Also creates 3 IC transfers (2 approved, 1 rejected) which mutate ownership 1m per test — NOT auto-rolled back; next tester should run seed_reset if strict integrity gate is required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file /app/backend/tests/test_m3_coa_per_pt_and_ic_transfer.py covers CoA per-PT + IC transfer JE. IMPORTANT: pytest must be invoked with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env exported (pytest.ini does not auto-load .env). IC transfer tests move ~2m stock KSC→KANDA total per run — run bash scripts/seed_reset.sh before re-running if integrity gate matters. Endpoint for rolls list is /api/inventory/rolls (NOT /api/inventory-rolls).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found; both features work as specified.
